Also 'Model' vs 'BaseModel' -> True, 'Serializer' vs 'BaseSerializer' -> True; control 'ExternalThing' vs 'BaseSchema' -> False.

The same-role-suffix exemption (added to kill inheritance false positives) treats `Schema` as same-role with `BaseSchema` because one is a suffix of the other. But `Base<Role>` wrapping the library's `<Role>` is THE dominant Python convention shape, so the exemption is guaranteed to swallow the exact mistake the rule exists to catch: skipping the repo's own base and inheriting the library base directly. Generalizes beyond Flask - I confirmed the same exemption fires for BaseModel/Model (SQLAlchemy, Pydantic) and BaseSerializer/Serializer (DRF).

The single most valuable convention chameleon derived in this cell (BaseSchema, 87.5%) is derived, written into conventions.md, marked enforced - and then structurally unenforceable.
